title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sumerian beer origin (Mesopotamia)
description: Uruk Mesopotamia ~4,000 BCE — Ninkasi hymn is the world's oldest written recipe. Beer was daily nutrition + ritual drink + wages.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sumerian beer origin (Mesopotamia)
description: Babylon — Hammurabi's Code (~1754 BCE) regulates tavern-keepers; ration lists record beer allocations.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Egyptian mass brewing
description: Cairo / Giza — Egyptian workers building pyramids received daily beer rations (4–5 litres).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Egyptian mass brewing
description: Luxor — documented brewery complexes associated with funerary complexes.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: European monastic brewing
description: Belgium — Trappist abbeys systematize brewing in Middle Ages; lambic, Flemish red, abbey styles all trace here.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: European monastic brewing
description: Munich Bavaria — 1516 Reinheitsgebot purity law limits beer to water, barley, hops.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: European monastic brewing
description: Plzeň Czech Republic — 1842 Pilsner Urquell creates the pale lager style that dominates global beer today.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: American immigration + craft revival
description: Midwest USA (Milwaukee/Chicago) — 19th-century German immigration builds the lager brewing industry.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: American immigration + craft revival
description: San Francisco Bay — Anchor Brewing + Sierra Nevada launch the US craft beer revolution in the 1970s–80s.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: American immigration + craft revival
description: Indianapolis — modern American craft scene, Anthony's home market.

LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Amsterdam / Heineken — third-largest brewer, global distribution from Dutch HQ.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Brussels / AB InBev — world's largest brewer; owns Budweiser, Stella, Corona, hundreds more.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: USA — world's #1 beer consumer + largest craft scene (~9,000 breweries).

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Beijing — China is now the world's #1 beer producer by volume (mostly Snow Beer, Tsingtao).
